examples.title: Amdahl's Law Speedup Calculator
examples.description: Program to calculate theoretical maximum speedup and actual speedup for given parallel fraction and processor count
examples.explanation: Implements Amdahl's Law formula. The parallel fraction P determines theoretical maximum speedup regardless of processor count. Real implementations include communication overhead which increases with processor count due to network latency and synchronization.

examples.title: MapReduce Data Parallelism Example
examples.description: Word count implementation demonstrating data parallelism in MapReduce paradigm
examples.explanation: Demonstrates data parallelism: identical map operation applied to different documents simultaneously across worker processes. The reduce phase then aggregates results. This pattern is fundamental to Hadoop MapReduce and scales horizontally across cloud clusters.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n/m + k) where n=documents, m=mappers, k=unique words

examples.title: Actor Model Implementation
examples.description: Simple actor-based concurrent system demonstrating message-passing concurrency
examples.explanation: Implements the Actor Model where each actor has private state and processes messages sequentially from its mailbox. No shared state or locks—concurrency emerges from actors processing messages independently. Used in Erlang/OTP, Akka for building fault-tolerant distributed systems.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m = mailbox queue size
